pattern: A flower is open for a person when it started no later and ended no earlier than the arrival time, so binary-search sorted start and end times to count starts-so-far minus already-ended.
hints: For an arrival time, a flower counts if its start is at most that time and its end is at least that time.
Sort all start times and all end times separately; the number open is the starts that have happened minus the ends that already finished.
For each person, binary-search the count of starts at most t and subtract the count of ends strictly before t.

edge_inputs.description: overlapping intervals queried at several times
duplicate arrival times and a point-interval flower
arrival just outside both ends of an interval
nested intervals all open at the inner time

stress.description: many intervals and query times spread over a wide range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    nf = rng.randint(1, min(size, 3000))
    npp = rng.randint(1, min(size, 3000))
    flowers = []
    for _ in range(nf):
        a = rng.randint(1, 100000)
        b = rng.randint(a, 100000)
        flowers.append([a, b])
    people = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(npp)]
    return [flowers, people]

solutions.python:
import bisect
class Solution:
    def fullBloomFlowers(self, flowers, people):
        starts = sorted(f[0] for f in flowers)
        ends = sorted(f[1] for f in flowers)
        res = []
        for t in people:
            started = bisect.bisect_right(starts, t)
            ended = bisect.bisect_left(ends, t)
            res.append(started - ended)
        return res
